judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is stronger: it catches all three expectations, including the nightly_export breakage (b), which Report 1 misses entirely.

report_1.evidence: the only place tenant_id appears in the SQL is as an output column in the SELECT list (line 14), never in the WHERE clause... any authenticated caller of any tenant can call GET /appointments/search?q=<term>&status=booked and receive matching appointments — including the notes field and tenant_id — belonging to every other tenant in the system

report_2.evidence: the string tenant_id appears in this function only as a column name in the SELECT list, never in the WHERE clause or the bound-params dict.

exports.py's nightly_export still calls build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id) with only two arguments — unchanged, because no commit on this branch touches exports.py... every call to nightly_export will now raise TypeError: build_appointment_query() missing 1 required positional argument: 'filters'.

report_1.evidence: status is typed AppointmentStatus in backend/app/schemas.py:5-8, a (str, Enum) with exactly three hardcoded members (booked, cancelled, completed), none containing quotes or SQL metacharacters... this specific line is not currently exploitable.

report_2.evidence: This isn't currently exploitable — FastAPI validates status against the AppointmentStatus str-Enum (schemas.py) before the function is ever called, so only "booked", "cancelled", or "completed" can reach it
